Title: Admit an active-visit patient to an inpatient ward via an Admission encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; an inpatient ward location with available beds exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient with an active visit | 2. Start the Admit to Inpatient form | 3. Select admitting ward and admitting provider | 4. Enter admission datetime and admission diagnosis | 5. Save the encounter
Test_Data: Ward=Male Ward; Provider=Dr Smith; AdmitDate=now; Dx=Pneumonia
Expected_Result: An Admission encounter is created, the patient is shown as Admitted to the selected ward, and the admission appears in the visit timeline with correct datetime and provider

Title: Block admission when patient has no active visit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record exists with no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Attempt to start the Admit to Inpatient form
Expected_Result: The system prevents admission and informs the user that an active visit is required before admitting

Title: Reject a future-dated admission datetime beyond the allowed window
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the Admit to Inpatient form | 2. Set admission datetime to a future date/time | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown and the admission is not saved because the datetime is in the future

Title: Reject admission datetime before the visit start datetime
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit started today
Test_Steps: 1. Start the Admit to Inpatient form | 2. Set admission datetime earlier than the visit start | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown; admission cannot precede the visit start datetime

Title: Prevent admitting a patient who is already admitted
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already has an active inpatient admission
Test_Steps: 1. Open the admitted patient | 2. Attempt to start a second Admit to Inpatient form | 3. Observe behavior
Expected_Result: The system blocks a duplicate admission and indicates the patient is already an inpatient

Title: Admission requires an admitting provider
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the Admit to Inpatient form | 2. Leave admitting provider blank | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown and the admission is not saved until a provider is selected

Title: Admit -> Transfer -> Discharge happy-path state transition
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient admitted to Ward A; Ward B has free capacity
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm state Admitted in Ward A | 2. Perform Transfer to Ward B | 3. Confirm state Admitted in Ward B | 4. Perform Discharge | 5. Confirm state Discharged
Expected_Result: Each transition is recorded as a discrete encounter with timestamps, the current state reflects the last action, and history shows Admit, Transfer, Discharge in order

Title: Disallow Transfer when patient is in Discharged state
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already discharged from current visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the discharged patient | 2. Attempt to perform a Transfer action
Expected_Result: The Transfer action is unavailable or blocked because no active inpatient state exists

Title: Disallow Discharge when patient was never admitted
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit but no admission
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient with an active outpatient visit | 2. Attempt to perform a Discharge action
Expected_Result: The Discharge action is blocked or unavailable because there is no active admission to discharge

Title: Cancel an erroneous admission and verify state reverts
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with cancel privilege; patient admitted in error
Test_Steps: 1. Open the admitted patient | 2. Void or cancel the admission encounter with a reason | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: The admission is voided, the patient no longer shows as Admitted, the bed is released, and the void reason is auditable

Title: Assign an available bed to an admitted patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; bed management configured; ward has at least one free bed
Test_Steps: 1. Open the admitted patient | 2. Open Assign Bed | 3. Select an available bed in the admitting ward | 4. Confirm assignment
Expected_Result: The bed is assigned to the patient, the bed status changes to Occupied, and the ward view shows the patient in that bed

Title: Prevent assigning a bed already occupied by another patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; bed ICU-03 already occupied by Patient X
Test_Steps: 1. Open Patient Y admitted to ICU | 2. Open Assign Bed | 3. Attempt to select occupied bed ICU-03
Expected_Result: The occupied bed is not selectable or assignment is rejected, preventing double-occupancy of a single bed

Title: Release a bed automatically on discharge
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient occupies bed ICU-03
Test_Steps: 1. Discharge the patient occupying ICU-03 | 2. Open the ward bed view
Expected_Result: On discharge the bed status returns to Available and the bed is free for reassignment

Title: Reassign bed on ward transfer releases the source bed
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient in Male Ward bed MW-05; ICU has free bed
Test_Steps: 1. Transfer patient from Male Ward to ICU | 2. Assign ICU bed | 3. Check source bed MW-05 status
Expected_Result: The source bed MW-05 is released to Available and the destination bed ICU-02 becomes Occupied

Title: Bed assignment respects gender-restricted ward configuration
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; Male Ward configured as male-only; Female Ward as female-only
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to assign a female patient to a male-only bed | 2. Attempt to assign a male patient to a female-only bed | 3. Attempt valid same-gender assignments
Expected_Result: Mismatched gender assignments are blocked or warned per configuration; matching assignments succeed

Title: Block admission to a ward at full capacity
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; target ward has all beds occupied
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to admit/assign a patient to the full ward | 2. Observe capacity behavior
Expected_Result: Admission/bed assignment to the full ward is blocked or routed to a waitlist with a capacity-full message

Title: Ward occupancy count updates on admit and discharge
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; ward shows 7/10 occupied
Test_Steps: 1. Admit one patient to the ward | 2. Verify occupancy 8/10 | 3. Discharge one patient | 4. Verify occupancy 7/10
Expected_Result: The occupancy counter increments on admission and decrements on discharge accurately and in real time

Title: Ward occupancy dashboard shows correct percentages across wards
Preconditions: Logged in as Ward Manager; multiple wards with known occupancy
Test_Steps: 1. Open the ward occupancy dashboard | 2. Compare displayed occupancy % to known counts
Expected_Result: Each ward shows the correct occupied/total and percentage, and totals aggregate correctly

Title: Transfer patient between wards records source and destination
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient admitted in Male Ward; ICU has capacity
Test_Steps: 1. Open the admitted patient | 2. Start Transfer form | 3. Select destination ICU and transfer datetime | 4. Enter transfer reason | 5. Save
Expected_Result: A Transfer encounter records source ward, destination ward, datetime and reason; current ward updates to ICU

Title: Reject transfer to the same ward the patient already occupies
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient currently in ICU
Test_Steps: 1. Start Transfer form | 2. Select destination = ICU (current ward) | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown; a no-op transfer to the same ward is not permitted

Title: Transfer datetime must not precede the admission datetime
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient admitted at 10:00 today
Test_Steps: 1. Start Transfer form | 2. Set transfer datetime to 09:00 same day | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown; a transfer cannot occur before admission

Title: Record a ward round progress note for an inpatient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient is currently admitted
Test_Steps: 1. Open the admitted patient | 2. Start the Ward Round / Progress Note form | 3. Enter subjective, objective, assessment and plan | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A ward round encounter is saved under the active inpatient visit with provider, datetime and note content

Title: Multiple ward rounds across days appear chronologically in the visit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient admitted for 3 days with one round per day
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient visit timeline | 2. Review ward round encounters
Expected_Result: All ward round encounters display in correct chronological order with distinct dates and providers

Title: Ward round entry requires the patient to be currently admitted
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient discharged earlier today
Test_Steps: 1. Open the discharged patient | 2. Attempt to start a ward round on the closed inpatient context
Expected_Result: The ward round cannot be filed against a discharged/closed admission, or it warns the encounter falls outside an active admission

Title: Place an inpatient medication order tied to the active admission
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with ordering privilege; patient admitted with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the admitted patient | 2. Open Orders | 3. Add a drug order with dose, route, frequency and duration | 4. Sign the order
Expected_Result: The inpatient order is created active, linked to the admission encounter/visit, and appears on the active orders list

Title: Discontinue an active inpatient order with a reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active inpatient drug order
Test_Steps: 1. Open active orders | 2. Discontinue the order | 3. Enter discontinue reason | 4. Sign
Expected_Result: The order transitions to Discontinued with a reason and stop datetime, and is no longer in active orders

Title: Revise an active inpatient order preserves order history chain
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active drug order
Test_Steps: 1. Revise the dose of the active order | 2. Sign the revision | 3. Review order history
Expected_Result: A new revision order is created linked to the previous order; history shows the original and revised orders with an audit trail

Title: Allergy conflict warning when ordering a drug the patient is allergic to
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient admitted with a documented penicillin allergy
Test_Steps: 1. Place an order for a penicillin-class drug | 2. Observe interaction/allergy alert | 3. Acknowledge or cancel
Expected_Result: An allergy alert is raised before signing; the clinician must acknowledge with a reason to override or cancel the order

Title: Active inpatient orders are auto-stopped on discharge per policy
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has active inpatient orders; auto-stop-on-discharge policy enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Discharge the patient | 2. Review the previously active inpatient orders
Expected_Result: Active inpatient orders are stopped/expired at discharge so they do not remain active after the admission ends

Title: Length of stay computes correctly from admission to discharge
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient admitted and discharged on known dates
Test_Steps: 1. Open the completed admission | 2. View computed length of stay
Expected_Result: Length of stay is computed as 4 days (or per defined LOS rule), matching the admit-to-discharge interval

Title: Same-day admission and discharge yields zero or one-day LOS per rule
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient admitted and discharged on the same calendar day
Test_Steps: 1. Admit at 08:00 | 2. Discharge at 14:00 same day | 3. View LOS
Expected_Result: LOS reflects the configured same-day rule (e.g., 0 days or minimum 1 day) consistently

Title: Current LOS for an in-progress admission counts from admit to now
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient still admitted (no discharge)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active admission | 2. View current length of stay
Expected_Result: Current LOS shows elapsed time from admission to the current datetime and updates as time passes

Title: Discharge an admitted patient with a discharge disposition
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient currently admitted
Test_Steps: 1. Open the admitted patient | 2. Start the Discharge form | 3. Select disposition and discharge datetime | 4. Enter discharge diagnosis | 5. Save
Expected_Result: A Discharge encounter is created, the patient state becomes Discharged, the bed is released, and the visit can be closed

Title: Discharge datetime must not precede the admission datetime
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient admitted at a known datetime
Test_Steps: 1. Start the Discharge form | 2. Set discharge datetime before admission | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown; discharge cannot occur before admission

Title: Warn or block discharge with active unresolved critical orders
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active critical/stat order pending
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to discharge the patient | 2. Observe warning regarding active orders | 3. Decide to proceed or cancel
Expected_Result: The system warns about active orders before discharge and requires acknowledgement, preventing silent loss of pending care

Title: Discharge Against Medical Advice (AMA) requires reason and captures disposition
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient currently admitted requesting to leave
Test_Steps: 1. Start the Discharge form | 2. Select disposition Against Medical Advice | 3. Save without reason then with reason
Expected_Result: AMA discharge requires a documented reason; once provided, the discharge is recorded with AMA disposition

Title: Record an inpatient death/deceased discharge disposition
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient currently admitted
Test_Steps: 1. Start the Discharge form | 2. Select disposition Deceased | 3. Enter date/time of death | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The discharge is recorded as Deceased, the patient deceased status/date is set, and the bed is released

Title: Generate a discharge summary containing required clinical sections
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient discharged with documented admission diagnosis, treatment and medications
Test_Steps: 1. Open the discharged admission | 2. Generate the discharge summary | 3. Review sections
Expected_Result: The discharge summary includes admission diagnosis, hospital course, procedures, discharge medications, follow-up and discharge instructions

Title: Block finalizing a discharge summary with missing mandatory sections
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; discharge summary draft missing follow-up instructions
Test_Steps: 1. Open the discharge summary draft | 2. Leave mandatory follow-up blank | 3. Attempt to finalize/sign
Expected_Result: Finalization is blocked with a validation message listing the missing mandatory sections

Title: Amend a finalized discharge summary creates an addendum, not a silent edit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a finalized discharge summary exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the finalized summary | 2. Add an amendment/addendum | 3. Sign | 4. Review document history
Expected_Result: An addendum is appended with author and timestamp; the original content is preserved and the change is auditable

Title: Detect a 30-day readmission for a recently discharged patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient discharged 10 days ago
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new admission for the same patient | 2. Observe readmission detection/flag
Expected_Result: The new admission is flagged as a 30-day readmission for reporting; admission still proceeds

Title: No readmission flag when prior discharge is outside the window
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient last discharged 45 days ago
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new admission for the same patient | 2. Observe readmission flag state
Expected_Result: The admission is not flagged as a 30-day readmission since it falls outside the configured window

Title: Nurse without admit privilege cannot perform admission
Preconditions: Logged in as Nurse role lacking the admit/ADT privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient with an active visit | 2. Attempt to start the Admit to Inpatient form
Expected_Result: The admit action is hidden or denied with an authorization error; no admission is created

Title: Prevent privilege escalation to discharge via direct REST call
Preconditions: API access as a user lacking discharge privilege; valid session token
Test_Steps: 1. Send a REST request to create a Discharge encounter for an admitted patient | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: The API returns 403 Forbidden and no discharge encounter is created, enforcing RBAC server-side not just in the UI

Title: Admission, transfer and discharge actions are written to the audit log
Preconditions: DB/audit access; clinician performs ADT actions
Test_Steps: 1. Admit, transfer and discharge a patient | 2. Query the audit/encounter log
Expected_Result: Each ADT action is recorded with actor, action type, patient, location and timestamp for later audit retrieval

Title: PHI access to the ward census list is logged per accessing user
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; user views ward census
Test_Steps: 1. View the ward census/patient list as User A | 2. Query access logs for the census view
Expected_Result: An access event is logged identifying the user, the ward census resource and timestamp to support HIPAA access auditing

Title: Represent admission as a FHIR Encounter with class IMP
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; patient admitted to an inpatient ward
Test_Steps: 1. GET the Encounter resource for the admission | 2. Inspect class, status, period and location
Expected_Result: The FHIR Encounter has class code IMP (inpatient encounter), status in-progress, a period.start, subject and the ward as a location reference

Title: FHIR Encounter status transitions to finished on discharge
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; patient discharged
Test_Steps: 1. GET the Encounter after discharge | 2. Inspect status and period.end
Expected_Result: After discharge the Encounter status is finished and period.end is populated with the discharge datetime

Title: FHIR Encounter.location uses status active then completed across transfers
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; patient transferred between two wards
Test_Steps: 1. GET the Encounter after a ward transfer | 2. Inspect Encounter.location entries
Expected_Result: Encounter.location lists both wards with appropriate location status (e.g., completed for source, active for destination) and periods

Title: Query active inpatients via FHIR Encounter search by class and status
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; multiple active and finished encounters exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Encounter?class=IMP&status=in-progress | 2. Inspect the returned bundle
Expected_Result: The bundle returns only active inpatient encounters and excludes outpatient and finished encounters

Title: Inbound HL7 v2 ADT^A01 admit message creates an inpatient admission
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured; matching patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 message with PV1 assigned patient location | 2. Process the message | 3. Verify admission
Expected_Result: The patient is admitted to the mapped ward/bed, an admission is created, and an ACK is returned

Title: HL7 ADT^A02 transfer message updates patient location
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured; patient currently admitted
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A02 transfer message with new PV1-3 location | 2. Process | 3. Verify ward
Expected_Result: The patient location updates to the new ward/bed per the A02 and a transfer is recorded

Title: HL7 ADT^A03 discharge message discharges the patient and sets disposition
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured; patient currently admitted
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A03 with PV1-36 discharge disposition | 2. Process | 3. Verify discharge
Expected_Result: The patient is discharged, disposition mapped from PV1-36, and the encounter closed with the discharge datetime

Title: Reject or NACK an ADT message referencing an unknown patient identifier
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured; identifier in message not present in system
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 with an unmatched PID-3 identifier | 2. Process the message
Expected_Result: The message is rejected with an application error/NACK (AE) and no admission is created from the unmatched identifier

Title: Two clinicians assigning the last free bed concurrently - only one succeeds
Preconditions: Two sessions; ward has exactly one free bed; bed management enabled
Test_Steps: 1. User A and User B both open Assign Bed for the same free bed | 2. Both submit nearly simultaneously
Expected_Result: Only one assignment succeeds; the other receives a conflict/already-occupied error, preventing double booking

Title: Pairwise admission across ward type, payer and admission type combinations
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; wards, payers and admission types configured
Test_Steps: 1. Execute admissions across pairwise combinations of ward type x payer x admission type | 2. Verify each admission and its derived attributes
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination admits successfully with correct ward, payer linkage and admission type, with no invalid combination accepted

Title: Ward census and bed board are operable and labelled for screen readers
Preconditions: Screen reader active; ward bed board open
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate the bed board grid using keyboard only | 2. Verify bed status and patient cells are announced | 3. Activate Assign Bed via keyboard
Expected_Result: All beds are keyboard reachable, status (Occupied/Available) and patient identity are announced with accessible names, and actions are operable without a mouse

Title: Exploratory session on rapid admit-transfer-discharge cycling for state consistency
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; test ward available
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly admit, transfer and discharge the same patient across several short cycles | 2. Interleave bed assignments and voids | 3. Observe state, bed status and history consistency
Expected_Result: No orphaned beds, no stuck Admitted states, history remains coherent, and counters/occupancy stay accurate throughout

Title: Bed-day charges accrue per night of stay for an inpatient admission
Preconditions: Billing module enabled; ward has a configured daily bed charge
Test_Steps: 1. Admit patient | 2. Advance/simulate multiple nights | 3. Discharge | 4. Review bed-day charges
Expected_Result: Bed-day charges accrue one charge per qualifying night per the LOS/charge rule, totalling correctly at discharge

Title: Voided admission encounter is soft-deleted and excluded from active census
Preconditions: DB access; admission encounter voided with a reason
Test_Steps: 1. Void an admission encounter | 2. Query the encounter table | 3. Query the active ward census
Expected_Result: The encounter row is retained with voided=true, void reason and voided_by populated, and the patient is excluded from the active census query

Title: Negative capacity is never displayed when discharges exceed admissions in edge data
Preconditions: Ward Manager; manipulated edge data with mismatched counts
Test_Steps: 1. Create edge scenario where discharge events exceed recorded admissions | 2. View occupancy counter
Expected_Result: Occupancy never renders a negative value; it floors at zero and surfaces a data inconsistency warning where applicable

Title: Discharge summary medications reconcile against active inpatient orders
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with active and discontinued inpatient drug orders being discharged
Test_Steps: 1. Generate the discharge summary | 2. Review the discharge medications section against order history
Expected_Result: Discharge medications reflect medication reconciliation (continued, changed, stopped) consistent with the inpatient order history
